name: Dual Dagger
description: Dual stereo filter with independent LPF and HPF cutoff controls. Four analog -24dB/oct filters, assignable resonance, stereo pan CV per filter type, and Link mode for 8th-order stereo bandpass.

controls.name: LPF Potentiometer
controls.description: Sets cutoff frequency of both low-pass filters (channels 1 and 2).

controls.name: Link Switch
controls.description: Links LPF to follow HPF, creating an 8th-order stereo bandpass. HPF = bandpass frequency, LPF = bandwidth.

controls.name: Resonance Potentiometer
controls.description: Shared resonance control. Applies to HPF, LPF, or both per switch settings. Jumper on back sets Lo (no self-oscillation) or Hi (self-oscillation allowed) range.

controls.name: HP Resonance Switch
controls.description: Engages resonance on the high-pass filters.
controls.detail: ON=resonance applied to HPF1 and HPF2

controls.name: LP Resonance Switch
controls.description: Engages resonance on the low-pass filters.
controls.detail: ON=resonance applied to LPF1 and LPF2

controls.name: HPF Potentiometer
controls.description: Sets cutoff frequency of both high-pass filters (channels 1 and 2). In Link mode, controls center frequency of bandpass.

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Audio input for channel 2. IN1 normalled to IN2.

inputs.name: LPF CV
inputs.description: CV input for LPF cutoff frequency.

inputs.name: HPF CV
inputs.description: CV input for HPF cutoff frequency.

inputs.name: PANLP CV
inputs.description: Pan CV for LPF: positive voltage opens LPF1 and closes LPF2 (opposite for negative).

inputs.name: PANHP CV
inputs.description: Pan CV for HPF: differentiates HPF cutoff between channels 1 and 2.

outputs.name: OUT1
outputs.description: Filtered audio output for channel 1 (HPF then LPF in series).

outputs.name: OUT2
outputs.description: Filtered audio output for channel 2 (HPF then LPF in series).

behaviors.name: Dual Stereo Filter
behaviors.description: Each audio channel passes through HPF then LPF in series. All four filters are -24dB/oct analog. LPF and HPF frequencies are identical across both channels for precise stereo tracking.

behaviors.name: Bandpass via Link
behaviors.description: LPF follows HPF. HPF control sets bandpass center frequency, LPF control sets bandwidth. Creates an 8th-order stereo bandpass with resonance assignable to HP and/or LP edges.

behaviors.name: Stereo Pan / Detuning
behaviors.description: Pan CV inputs offset the cutoff frequency of channel 1 vs channel 2 in opposite directions. Positive voltage to PANLP opens LPF1 and closes LPF2, creating stereo filter divergence.

behaviors.name: Self-Oscillation VCO
behaviors.trigger: Hi jumper + Resonance max + HPF CV
behaviors.description: With Hi jumper set and resonance at maximum, the HPF section self-oscillates as a sine-wave VCO tracking V/Oct over several octaves.

patchIdeas.name: Stereo bandpass sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Link ON · LFO sweeps HPF CV (bandpass center) · LPF sets bandwidth · resonance on both edges.
steps: Flip the Link switch (B, top-left) ON — LPF now follows HPF as bandwidth.
Flip HP Resonance switch (D) ON and LP Resonance switch (E) ON so both edges resonate.
Set Resonance pot (C) to about 2 o'clock; set LPF pot (A) about 9 o'clock (narrow band) and HPF pot (F) at 12 o'clock.
Patch your stereo source: VCO L (Out) → IN1 (jack 5), VCO R (Out) → IN2 (jack 6).
Patch a slow LFO (~0.1 Hz triangle) → HPF CV (jack 3) — sweeps the bandpass center.
Patch OUT1 (jack 6, lower) → Mixer L, OUT2 → Mixer R.
Widen the LPF pot to taste — further open = wider band; almost closed = razor-thin pass.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 / OUT2 — stereo audio inside the swept band, ±5V; mostly silent outside the passband.
patchIdeas.hear: A wah-like spectral window that slides up and down the stereo field; both edges ring as the LFO sweeps, like a vocal vowel scanning the spectrum.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* Link: ON
* HP Resonance: ON
* LP Resonance: ON
- VCO L (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN1)
- VCO R (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN2)
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (HPF CV) // sweeps bandpass center
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (L)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Dual resonant peak
patchIdeas.patch: Mono noise → IN1 · sum OUT1+OUT2 · PANLP CV splits the two LP peaks for double-resonant comb.
steps: Leave Link switch (B) OFF; flip LP Resonance switch (E) ON.
Set Resonance pot (C) to about 3 o'clock (rich peak, no self-osc with Lo jumper).
Set LPF pot (A) at 12 o'clock; HPF pot (F) fully counter-clockwise (out of the way).
Patch Noise (Out) → IN1 (jack 5). Leave IN2 unpatched — IN1 is normalled to IN2.
Patch a slow LFO (bipolar, ±5V) → PANLP CV (jack 4) — pushes LPF1 up while LPF2 goes down.
Patch OUT1 → Mixer CH 1, OUT2 → Mixer CH 2 (or sum to mono).
Adjust LFO depth to control how far apart the two resonant peaks separate.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 / OUT2 — two filtered noise streams, each with its own moving resonant peak around the LPF cutoff, ±5V.
patchIdeas.hear: Two whistling formants drifting apart and back together over a noise bed — like two bottles being blown at slowly diverging pitches.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* LP Resonance: ON
- Noise (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN1) // IN2 normalled from IN1
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANLP CV) // splits the two LP peaks
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (CH1)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (CH2)

patchIdeas.name: Sine VCO voice with stereo detune
patchIdeas.patch: Hi jumper · resonance max · HPF self-oscillates tracking V/Oct · LPF smooths · PANHP detunes the two channels.
steps: Power down, set the back-panel jumper to Hi (allows self-oscillation), power up.
Flip HP Resonance switch (D) ON; turn Resonance pot (C) fully clockwise — HPFs sing as sine VCOs.
Set LPF pot (A) about 2 o'clock so the LPF stays well above the sine pitch (clean tone).
Patch Keyboard (Pitch) → HPF CV (jack 3) — V/Oct tracking over several octaves (set HPF pot to mid for a useful range).
Patch a slow LFO (small depth) → PANHP CV (jack 4) — detunes the channel-1 sine vs channel-2 sine.
Patch OUT1 → Output L, OUT2 → Output R.
Tune by ear at one keyboard note: rotate HPF pot until the pitch matches a reference oscillator at the same V/Oct.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 / OUT2 — two slightly detuned sine waves tracking V/Oct, ±5V; LPF removes any harmonic crud above the sine.
patchIdeas.hear: A warm stereo sine voice with a slow chorus shimmer from the PANHP detune — Juno-style fattened mono lead spread across the field.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* HP Resonance: ON · Hi jumper · Res max
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Dual Dagger (HPF CV) // V/Oct tracking
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANHP CV) // detune channel 2
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Output (L)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Resonant kick drum
patchIdeas.patch: Hi jumper · self-oscillating HPF pinged by a fast decay envelope on HPF CV — stereo kick with PANHP detune.
steps: Set the back-panel jumper to Hi; flip HP Resonance switch (D) ON; turn Resonance pot (C) fully clockwise so HPF self-oscillates.
Set HPF pot (F) low (around 9 o'clock, ~60-80 Hz body pitch); set LPF pot (A) about 1 o'clock to tame the click.
Patch a fast AD envelope (5-10 ms attack, 200-400 ms decay, ~5V peak) → HPF CV (jack 3) — each trigger pings the pitch up and back down.
Patch a small fixed offset or slow LFO → PANHP CV (jack 4) for a touch of stereo width.
Trigger the envelope from your clock or sequencer.
Patch OUT1 → Mixer L, OUT2 → Mixer R.
Shorten the decay for a tighter kick; raise HPF pot for tom-style pitches.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 / OUT2 — pitched percussive thumps following the envelope, ±5V; silent between hits.
patchIdeas.hear: A bass-drum thump with a pitch sweep down — short attack, body that drops in pitch as the envelope decays, with subtle stereo placement.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* HP Resonance: ON · Hi jumper · Res max
* Link: OFF
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Dual Dagger (HPF CV) // pings pitch
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANHP CV) // stereo width
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (L)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Doubled mono filter (parallel HP+LP peaks)
patchIdeas.patch: Mono source → IN1 · both HP and LP resonance ON · sum OUT1+OUT2 for a fat double-peak sound.
steps: Leave Link switch (B) OFF; flip both HP Resonance (D) and LP Resonance (E) ON.
Set Resonance pot (C) about 2 o'clock (resonant but not screaming).
Set LPF pot (A) about 11 o'clock and HPF pot (F) about 2 o'clock — two peaks in different parts of the spectrum.
Patch a harmonically rich source (sawtooth VCO) → IN1 (jack 5). Leave IN2 unpatched (IN1 normalled).
Patch your envelope or LFO → LPF CV (jack 1) to sweep the LP peak.
Patch a second slow LFO → HPF CV (jack 3) to drift the HP peak independently.
Sum OUT1 + OUT2 to a single mixer channel for a thick mono signal with two independent resonant formants.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 + OUT2 summed — one mono signal carrying two resonant peaks (one LP, one HP), ±5V each.
patchIdeas.hear: A double-peaked vocal-formant timbre, like 'oo' and 'ee' vowels stacked, with each peak swept by its own modulator.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* HP Resonance: ON · LP Resonance: ON · Link: OFF
- Saw VCO (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN1) // IN2 normalled
- Envelope (Out) >> Dual Dagger (LPF CV)
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (HPF CV)
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (CH1)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (CH1) // sum mono

patchIdeas.name: Mid-Side stereo width filter
patchIdeas.patch: Encode L/R to M/S externally (e.g. SumDif) · M → IN1, S → IN2 · PANLP offsets cutoff between mid and side for width control.
steps: Leave Link switch (B) OFF; both Resonance switches OFF for a clean tone.
Set LPF pot (A) about 2 o'clock; HPF pot (F) fully counter-clockwise (out of the way).
Use an external M/S encoder (Shakmat SumDif or similar): feed your stereo L/R into it; route Mid out → IN1 (jack 5) and Side out → IN2 (jack 6).
Patch a slow LFO → PANLP CV (jack 4) — opens LPF1 (mid) while closing LPF2 (side), and vice versa.
Take OUT1 (filtered Mid) → external decoder Mid in; OUT2 (filtered Side) → decoder Side in.
Decoder L/R → mixer L/R for the final stereo signal.
Adjust LFO depth to control how dramatically the mid vs side spectrum diverges over time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT1 — filtered Mid signal; OUT2 — filtered Side signal; both ±5V, ready for M/S decoding.
patchIdeas.hear: Stereo width that breathes: when the Side is darker than Mid the image collapses to centre; when Side is brighter than Mid the image flares wide.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* Link: OFF · Resonance: OFF
- Stereo (L) -> SumDif Encode (In L)
- Stereo (R) -> SumDif Encode (In R)
- SumDif Encode (Mid) -> Dual Dagger (IN1)
- SumDif Encode (Side) -> Dual Dagger (IN2)
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANLP CV) // breathes width
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> SumDif Decode (Mid)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> SumDif Decode (Side)
- SumDif Decode (Out L) -> Mixer (L)
- SumDif Decode (Out R) -> Mixer (R)
